output: Completed T2. The lifecycle migration was copied into the workspace Supabase migrations directory, applied to the linked Banodoco project, confirmed in remote migration history, and verified through Supabase REST column selection. Updated the migration staging README and wrote the batch checkpoint.
commands_run: supabase projects list && supabase migration list --linked
find .. -maxdepth 3 -type d -name migrations -path '*supabase*' -print && find ../supabase -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort | tail -20

set -a; source .env; set +a; psql "$SUPABASE_CONNECTION_STRING" -Atc "select column_name || ':' || data_type || ':' || is_nullable || ':' || coalesce(column_default, '') from information_schema.columns where table_schema = 'public' and table_name = 'live_update_watchlist' and column_name in ('expires_at','next_revisit_at','revisit_count','origin_reason','evidence') order by column_name;"

set -a; source .env; set +a; curl -sS -o /tmp/live_update_watchlist_lifecycle_check.json -w '%{http_code}\n' "$SUPABASE_URL/rest/v1/live_update_watchlist?select=expires_at,next_revisit_at,revisit_count,origin_reason,evidence&limit=1" -H "apikey: $SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY" -H "Authorization: Bearer $SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY"; head -c 500 /tmp/live_update_watchlist_lifecycle_check.json
deviations: Direct psql information_schema verification could not run because local DNS resolution failed for db.ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf.supabase.co; verified the columns through Supabase REST instead, which returned HTTP 200.
A git status command that included ../supabase failed because that path is outside the brain-of-bndc repository; file presence was verified with ls instead.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified U1 remained satisfied: `supabase projects list` showed the linked Banodoco project `ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf`, and `supabase migration list --linked` connected to remote history.
